brief: Opportunities are heavily concentrated in Brooklyn R7A and R7-2 zones leveraging 485-x tax exemptions paired with Section 8 VPS rents, delivering cash-on-cash returns between 11.2% and 13.7%. Across 921 qualified parcels, city-owned sites show strong upside with walk-away land values substantially higher than estimated land costs. By contrast, Bronx ELLA/4% LIHTC program plays show negative land headroom, requiring competitive subsidy awards to generate developer fees.

targets.play: Develop a 12-unit residential project in an R7A zone leveraging a 485-x tax exemption with Section 8 VPS rents to achieve a 13.7% cash-on-cash return.
targets.negotiation: Estimated land cost is $591,738 versus a walk-away land price of $1,588,048, providing $996,310 in price headroom.
targets.risk: Navigating the public HPD disposition process for city-owned land along with verifying R7A zoning and site conditions.

targets.play: Build a 10-unit project in an R7-2 zone utilizing 485-x and Section 8 VPS rents targeting a 12.7% cash-on-cash return.
targets.negotiation: Estimated land cost is $569,141 against a maximum walk-away land cap of $1,525,802 ($956,661 headroom).
targets.risk: City-owned HPD/EDC RFP disposition requirements and site condition diligence.

targets.play: Construct a 14-unit building in an R7A zone using the 485-x tax exemption and Section 8 VPS rents yielding 12.4% cash-on-cash.
targets.negotiation: Estimated land cost is $690,612 against a walk-away land threshold of $1,760,767 ($1,070,155 headroom).
targets.risk: HPD disposition RFP process for city-owned parcels and zoning verification.

targets.play: Deliver a 14-unit project in Queens under R6A zoning via 485-x and Section 8 VPS rents targeting an 11.2% cash-on-cash return.
targets.negotiation: Estimated land cost is $622,014 compared to a walk-away land valuation of $1,531,605 ($909,591 headroom).
targets.risk: HPD disposition timeline, environmental site condition, and Queens zoning verification.

process_note: These initial screening estimates require complete diligence, and because the top targets are city-owned, acquisition depends on HPD/EDC disposition RFPs rather than standard private purchases.
disclaimer: AI interpretation of the engine's real screening output. Estimates pending diligence — not investment advice.
